initialBaseline.note: The inventory count was captured before correcting two upload-open status collisions and the App forbidden namespace.

preContractCorrections.legacyBehavior: ADMIN_FILE_OPEN_FAILED represented both upload parsing and stored-object reads.
preContractCorrections.stableBehavior: ADMIN_FILE_UPLOAD_OPEN_FAILED represents upload parsing; ADMIN_FILE_OPEN_FAILED represents stored-object reads.

preContractCorrections.legacyBehavior: APP_FILE_OPEN_FAILED represented both upload parsing and stored-object reads.
preContractCorrections.stableBehavior: APP_FILE_UPLOAD_OPEN_FAILED represents upload parsing; APP_FILE_OPEN_FAILED represents stored-object reads.

preContractCorrections.legacyBehavior: ADMIN_FORBIDDEN was emitted from App authorization paths.
preContractCorrections.stableBehavior: ADMIN_FORBIDDEN is Admin-owned and APP_FORBIDDEN is App-owned.
AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS uses the non-enumerating message invalid credentials.
APP_PHONE_INVALID_REQUEST uses the stable message invalid app phone request.

preContractCorrections.legacyBehavior: ADMIN_RESOURCE_LIFECYCLE_CONFLICT and ADMIN_RESOURCE_INVALID_RECORD could expose adapter errors.
preContractCorrections.stableBehavior: Both codes use fixed redacted public messages; private causes remain internal.

preContractCorrections.legacyBehavior: App file metadata paths could emit ADMIN_RESOURCE_* codes.
preContractCorrections.stableBehavior: App paths use App-owned file codes and never depend on Admin resource code aliases.
